blocks:
Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۲)
The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, All-wise. (2)
إِنَّ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۳)
In the heavens and the earth there are indeed signs for the faithful, (3)

Key Points

Allah is Wise, «الحكیم» (The Wise). His Book is also wise, «یس والقرآن الحكیم» (1). His Prophet is also a teacher of wisdom. «یعلّمهم الكتاب والحكمة» (2). The Book of God and His creation are visible to everyone, but only the pious benefit from His Book, «هُدىً للمتّقین» (3), and only the believers are reminded of God by His creation. «لآیات للمؤمنین»

1) Yasin, 1 - 2. 2) Al-Baqarah, 129. 3) Al-Baqarah, 2.

Messages

1- The Quran is composed of these very letters of the alphabet available to everyone, and it was revealed gradually. «حم، تنزیل الكتاب»

2- Sending down the Book and the law is among the greatest divine blessings. Therefore, its mention precedes the blessing of the heavens and the earth. «تنزیل الكتاب»

3- In the method of propagation, the status of the Quran must first be clarified, and then its reminders and commands should be followed. «تنزیل... العزیز الحكیم»

4- The Quran possesses both authenticity, «من اللّه» (from Allah), and immunity (protection). «العزیز»

5- The system of legislation (Sharī‘ah) and the system of creation (Takwīn) originate from a single source and are in harmony with each other. «تنزیل الكتاب... انّ فى السموات...»

6- Studying nature for the purpose of knowing God is encouraged by the Quran. «انّ فى السموات و الارض لآیات للمؤمنین»

7- Not only the verses of the Quran, but also the pages of the book of nature, are signs and tokens for the believers. «السموات و الارض لآیات للمؤمنین»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Jathiyah

This Surah was revealed in Mecca and contains thirty-seven verses. The name of this Surah was chosen based on its 28th verse, and Jathiyah refers to the state of someone who has fallen to their knees. In the first and last verses of this Surah, Allah is described with the two attributes of Al-Aziz (The Almighty) and Al-Hakim (The Wise). Most of the contents of this Surah concern the greatness of the Quran, proofs of Monotheism (Tawhid), responses to materialists, the history of some previous nations, warnings to the misguided, an invitation to pardon and forgiveness, and references to the scenes of the Day of Judgment.

Ayahs

وَفِى خَلْقِكُمْ وَمَا يَبُثُّ مِن دَآبَّةٍ ءَايَـٰتٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (٤)
There are signs for people who have certainty in your own creation and in animals, He scatters abroad. (4)
وَٱخْتِلَـٰفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مِن رِّزْقٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَتَصْرِيفِ ٱلرِّيَـٰحِ ءَايَـٰتٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (٥)
There are signs for people who exercise their reason in the alternation of night and day, in the provision that Allah sends down from the sky, with which He revives the earth after its death, and in the changing of the winds. (5)

Key Points

Verses 3 to 5, which discuss the signs of creation, address a specific group at the end of each verse: once the people of faith, once the people of certainty, and another time the people of intellect. This is perhaps because the path of faith is not separate from the path of reason, and both lead humanity to certainty. Of course, the matter is more precise and extensive in the creation of humans and moving creatures, and careful consideration and reflection upon it lead to certainty.

The regulation of the Earth's rotation and the succession of night and day is another sign of Divine power and wisdom. If this rotation did not exist, or if it were too slow or too fast, life would be paralyzed. Therefore, the importance of these blessings becomes clearer when they are taken away from humanity.

Wind, rain, night, and day play a crucial role in the life of earthly creatures, and they are mentioned together in one verse.

Creation is one matter, and managing and providing sustenance is another. The sustenance of every creature has been regulated since the beginning of creation.

Allah has sent down the Book (the Quran) to revive hearts and rain to revive the soil (or clay).

The meaning of the variation (اختلاف) of night and day is either the appearance of one following the other, or the difference in the hours of night and day, where sometimes the day is long and the night is short, and sometimes the reverse is true.

In the wind, there are three important blessings: moving the clouds, pollinating plants, and purifying the air.

Throughout the entire Quran, whenever the creation of night and day is mentioned, the name of the night precedes it. «الیل والنهار»

Messages

1- The entire existence is a book for knowing Allah. The heavens, humans, and animals all lead us to a single goal. «و فى خلقكم و مایبثّ من دابّة»

2- One of the Divine blessings is the dispersal of moving creatures; otherwise, human life would be paralyzed. «یبثّ من دابّة»

3- The creation of the heavens and the earth is one aspect, the creation of all moving creatures is another aspect, and the creation of humanity is yet another aspect. «خلقكم و ما یبث» (The name of humanity is mentioned separately and before other moving creatures, which indicates its importance.)

4- Studying the cosmic order is the path to achieving certainty. «لقوم یوقنون»

5- The sky is the source of human sustenance. «من السماء من رزق»

6- The Earth experiences death and life, and this is constantly repeated (with autumn and spring). «فاحیا به الارض بعد موتها»

7- The movement of the winds is not accidental; rather, it is based on a Divine plan. «تصریف الریاح»

8- The Book of Existence is full of various signs, the comprehension of which requires reflection and thought. «آیات لقوم یعقلون»

Ayah

تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ فَبِأَىِّ حَدِيثٍۭ بَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ وَءَايَـٰتِهِۦ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٦)
These are the signs of Allah that We recite for you in truth. So what discourse will they believe after Allah and His signs? (6)

Messages

1- We must place the means of recognition before the people so that the proof (Hujjah) against them is completed. «تلك آیات اللّه»

2- The Quran was revealed to the Prophet using these very words. «نتلوها علیك»

3- The one who is meant to build (reform) the people must first be built (reformed) himself. «نتلوها علیك»

4- The Divine verses are based on truth (Haqq); weak speech, superstition, exaggeration, imagination, and the like have no place in them. «بالحقّ»

5- Human free will causes man to follow another path despite the existence of the Divine path. «فباىّ حدیث... یؤمنون»

6- The deviation of man after all those truthful verses and recitations is astonishing and deserving of reproach. «فباى حدیث بعد اللّه و آیاته یؤمنون»

7- Those who hear the Divine verses but are not guided have an internal problem, meaning that Allah’s verses are neither false nor ambiguous. «نتلوها علیك بالحقّ... فباىّ حدیث... یؤمنون»

Ayahs

وَيْلٌ لِّكُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍ (۷)
Woe to every sinful liar, (7)
يَسْمَعُ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ يُصِرُّ مُسْتَكْبِرًا كَأَن لَّمْ يَسْمَعْهَا ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۸)
who hears the signs of Allah being recited to him, yet persists arrogantly as if he had not heard them. So inform him of a painful punishment. (8)
وَإِذَا عَلِمَ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا شَيْـًٔا ٱتَّخَذَهَا هُزُوًا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (۹)
Should he learn anything about Our signs, he takes them in derision. For such there is a humiliating punishment. (9)

Key Points

"Affāk" (أفّاك) derives from "Ifk" (اِفك), meaning someone who tells many lies. "Athīm" (أثیم) derives from "Ithm" (اِثم), referring to someone who is highly sinful. "Wayl" (ویل), which is an imprecation meaning "destruction be upon him," appears 27 times in the Quran. This word has been used in various and numerous instances in the Quran; it is used concerning cultural betrayers, those who write things with their own hands and then attribute them to Allah: «فویل للّذین یكتبون الكتاب بایدیهم ثمّ یقولون هذا من عند اللّه»(4). It is also used concerning economic oppressors, such as those who give short measure: «ویل للمطفّفین»(5). And concerning those who are ideologically deviant, such as the disbelievers: «ویل للكافرین»(6), and the polytheists: «ویل للمشركین»(7). And also concerning those who mock the divine saints and the truths: «ویل لكلّ هُمَزةٍ لُمزة»(8). Divine punishment is commensurate with the crime. The punishment for someone who hears the Prophet's words but acts as if he did not hear them, thereby hurting the Prophet's heart, is a painful torment (ʿadhāb alīm). And the punishment for someone who insults through mockery is a humiliating torment (ʿadhāb muhīn). The word bishārah (glad tidings) is used for good news, but in these verses, Allah has called the promise of torment for the disbelievers "glad tidings." It is as if the best news for the obstinate opponents is the news of torment.

4) Al-Baqarah, 79. 5) Al-Mutaffifīn, 1. 6) Ibrāhīm, 2. 7) Fuṣṣilat, 6. 8) Al-Humazah, 1.

Messages

1- The word of truth must be conveyed to everyone, even the sinners. «افّاك اثیم... تتلى علیه»

2- First propagation, then threat. The Quran says "destruction be upon him" to someone only after His verses have been conveyed to him. «یسمع آیات اللّه... كان لم یسمعها... عذاب الیم»

3- Worse than ignoring the truth is insisting upon it and possessing the spirit of arrogance (istikbār). «یصرّ مستكبراً»

4- The sign of arrogance (takabbur) is indifference to the word of truth. «مستكبراً كان لم یسمعها»

5- The root of fault-finding and mockery, in many cases, is the lack of comprehensive and sufficient information. «فاذا علم من آیاتنا شیئاً اتّخذها هزواً» Yes, if knowledge becomes deep and extensive, mockery vanishes.

6- Since mockery involves insult, the punishment for mockers is also a humiliating torment. «هزواً... عذاب مهین»

Ayah

مِّن وَرَآئِهِمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَلَا يُغْنِى عَنْهُم مَّا كَسَبُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا مَا ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٠)
Ahead of them is hell, and neither what they have earned, nor what they had taken as guardians besides Allah will avail them in any way, and there is a great punishment for them. (10)

Key Points

Wara' (وراء) means both behind and in front. In this Ayah, wara' means in front, just as we read in Surah Al-Kahf, Ayah 79: «و كان ورائهم ملك یاخذ كلّ سفینة غصباً» Khidr told Moses: I damaged the ship because ahead of them was a tyrannical king who forcibly confiscated sound ships, and I damaged the ship so that it would be defective and he would not confiscate it. We also read in Surah Al-Mu'minun, Ayah 100: When death comes to one of them, he says, "My Lord, send me back so that I may do righteousness." They are answered: Never. Then He says: «من ورائهم برزخ» These individuals have the Barzakh ahead of them after death until the time of Resurrection when they are raised. Here, too, the word wara' means in front. In this Ayah and the preceding Ayat, various types of punishment have been mentioned: 'Adhāb Alīm (a painful punishment), 'Adhāb Mahīn (a humiliating punishment), and 'Adhāb 'Aẓīm (a tremendous punishment).

Messages

1- The polytheists will be alone and defenseless on the Day of Judgment. «لا یغنى عنهم ما كسبوا شیئا»

2- The reliance of the arrogant is either on their possessions or their friends, neither of which will be effective on the Day of Judgment. «لا یغنى عنهم ما كسبوا شیئاً و لا ما اتّخذوا من دون اللّه اولیاء»

3- The Quran foretells the disgrace and humiliation of mockers and violators of sanctity in this world. «اتّخذها هزواً لهم عذاب مهین من ورائهم جهنّم» (Since 'Adhāb Mahīn [humiliating punishment] is mentioned alongside Hell, the apparent meaning is humiliation and disgrace in this world.)

4- Worldly means and wealth will be ineffective on the Day of Judgment. «لا یغنى عنهم ما كسبوا شیئا»

5- Attention to Hell can be the best deterrent against arrogance and persistence in sin. «أثیم - بصیر... لهم عذاب عظیم»

Ayah

هَـٰذَا هُدًى ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مِّن رِّجْزٍ أَلِيمٌ (۱۱)
This is [true] guidance, and there is a painful punishment due to defilement for those who defy the signs of their Lord. (11)

Key Points

The word "رِجز" (Rijz) means rijs (filth) and pollution. This means that the disbelievers will be tormented by means of polluted and repulsive materials.

Messages

1- The Quran is entirely guidance: its commands and prohibitions, its stories and its parables. «هذا هُدىً»

2- Divine punishment occurs after the completion of the proof (or: after the argument has been fully established). «هذا هُدىً والّذین كفروا...»

Ayah

۞ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلْبَحْرَ لِتَجْرِىَ ٱلْفُلْكُ فِيهِ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۱۲)
It is Allah who disposed the sea for you[r benefit] so that the ships may sail in it by His command, that you may seek of His bounty and that you may give thanks. (12)

Messages

1- The actions of Allah are purposeful, wise, and aimed at benefiting humanity. «سخّر لكم، لتجرى البحر لتبتغوا - لعلّكم تشكرون»

2- Material benefits must serve as a prelude to the divine relationship. «لتجرى... لتبتغوا... لعلّكم تشكرون»

3- The subjection of the seas and the influence of natural factors are under the command of God. «بامره»

4- Effort toward earning a livelihood and achieving welfare is a value. «لتبتغوا»

5- Although bounty and mercy originate from Allah, man must also be striving and making effort to attain them. «لتبتغوا من فضله»

6- Man has no claim upon Allah; everything that exists is His favor. «من فضله»

7- Paying attention to blessings revives the spirit of gratitude in man. «سخّر لكم... لعلّكم تشكرون»

8- The oceans are submissive to man; perhaps man should also become submissive to God. «لعلكم تشكرون»

9- Man is free and possesses free will. «لعلّكم تشكرون» (The word «لعلّ» [perhaps] is not a sign of doubt on the part of Allah, but rather a sign of man's free will, indicating that he can be grateful or ungrateful.)

Ayah

وَسَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا مِّنْهُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۱۳)
He has disposed for you[r benefit] whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth; all is from Him. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. (13)

Messages

1- The entire existence is aligned for the benefit of mankind. «سخّر لكم ما فى السموات...»

2- When we gain mastery over existence, we should not become arrogant, for everything that exists is from Him. «جمیعاً منه»

3- The harmony of the components of existence towards a single direction is a sign of the Creator's Oneness. «جمیعاً منه»

4- Reflecting upon Divine blessings is a form of gratitude towards Him. «تشكرون... لقوم یتفكّرون»

5- Allah has invited and encouraged mankind towards reflection. «لقوم یتفكّرون»

6- In the subjugation of existence for mankind, there are many signs for those who reflect. «لآیات لقوم یتفكّرون» (Perhaps the relationship between reflection and subjugation is that a power is inherent in man, enabling him to utilize all manifestations of nature for his own benefit, and this power is activated through thought.)

7- Cultivate both emotion and develop thought. «تشكرون - یتفكّرون»

Ayah

قُل لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَغْفِرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ أَيَّامَ ٱللَّهِ لِيَجْزِىَ قَوْمًۢا بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۱٤)
Say to the faithful to forgive those who do not expect Allah’s days, that He may [Himself] requite every people for what they used to earn. (14)

Key Points

The meaning of Ayyām Allāh (the Days of Allah) refers to historical days in which great transformations occur, either manifesting Allah's wrath against the oppressors or His mercy towards the believers. The Muslims in Mecca were afflicted by the enmity and harassment of the Meccan polytheists. They considered retaliation, but Allah commanded His Prophet to tell the Muslims not to rush, as they were not yet strong, and to leave the polytheists to Him.

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to teach the believers the method of dealing with the disbelievers. «قل للّذین آمنوا...»

2- Forgiveness and magnanimity are prerequisites of faith. «للّذین آمنوا یغفروا»

3- Achieving lofty religious goals requires patience and avoidance of haste. «للّذین آمنوا یغفروا»

4- Belief that the disbelievers will face divine punishment fosters forgiveness and patience in a person. «یغفروا... لیجزى قوماً...»

5- Belief affects action; since the disbelievers have no hope in the Day of Judgment, they resort to committing evil deeds. «لا یرجون... یكسبون»

6- Man is bound by his own deeds. «لیجزى... بما كانوا یكسبون»

7- Punishment is for the evil deeds that man knowingly commits. «بما كانوا یكسبون»

8- What causes divine wrath is the persistence and insistence upon deviation and transgression. «كانوا یكسبون»

Ayah

مَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَنْ أَسَآءَ فَعَلَيْهَا ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ (۱٥)
Whoever acts righteously, it is for his own soul, and whoever does evil, it is to its own detriment, then you will be brought back to your Lord. (15)

Key Points

| The Quran has repeatedly referred to this principle through various statements: that the result of one's action returns to the person himself, as He says: «و مَن یشكر فانّما یشكر لنفسه و من كفر فانّ اللّه غنىّ حمید»(9) Whoever is grateful, he is grateful for his own soul, and whoever is ungrateful, it is to his own detriment. «مَن اهتدى فلنفسه و مَن ضلّ فانما یضلّ علیها»(10) Whoever accepts guidance, it is for his own benefit, and whoever goes astray, it is to his own detriment.

9) Luqman, 12. 10) Az-Zumar, 41.

Messages

1- The system of Divine reward and punishment is just and based on the good and bad performance of the human being himself. «مَن عمل صالحاً... و مَن اساء»

2- Allah has no need for human good deeds. «عمل صالحاً فلنفسه»

3- Human beings are equal before the Divine law. (The human, whoever he may be, and the action, whatever it may be, will be subject to reward and punishment.) «مَن عمل صالحا... و مَن اساء فعلیها»

4- God created man free. «مَن عمل صالحا... مَن اساء»

5- The reason for the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is Divine Justice. «مَن عمل صالحا... مَن اساء... الى ربّكم ترجعون»

6- The Resurrection is a hope for the righteous and a warning for the wrongdoers. «الى ربّكم ترجعون»

7- The Day of Judgment is compulsory and not by human choice. «تُرجعون»

8- Divine reward and punishment are requirements of His Lordship (Rububiyyah). «الى ربّكم ترجعون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحُكْمَ وَٱلنُّبُوَّةَ وَرَزَقْنَـٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ وَفَضَّلْنَـٰهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦)
Certainly We gave the Children of Israel the Book, judgement and prophethood and We provided them with all the good things. We gave them an advantage over all the nations, (16)

Key Points

Since «رزقناهم» (We provided them sustenance) comes after «آتینا» (We gave), we understand that desirable sustenance only has meaning alongside Divine law, leadership, and government. Otherwise, income earned outside of Divine law, even if abundant, will not be pure (ṭayyib). The Children of Israel were unable to preserve the Divine favors mentioned in this Ayah. Consequently, their greatness and honor were transformed into humiliation and a curse due to their disobedience and transgression. «لعن الّذین كفروا من بنى اسرائیل على لسان داود» (11)

11) Al-Ma'idah, 78.

Messages

1- The superiority of a society depends on its culture and economy derived from Divine law. «آتینا... الكتاب... رزقنا... فضلنا»

2- Everything we possess is from Allah, so we must not become arrogant. «اتینا... رزقنا... فضّلنا»

3- Spiritual blessings are prioritized. In this Ayah, the Book, judgment, and prophethood are mentioned before sustenance and provision. «آتینا... رزقنا»

4- Religious government has a long history. «الكتاب والحكم و النبوّة»

5- A culture is complete when its law is Divine, its leader is infallible (ma'ṣūm), and its judgment and government are just. «الكتاب والحكم و النبوّة»

Ayah

وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم بَيِّنَـٰتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ ۖ فَمَا ٱخْتَلَفُوٓا۟ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعِلْمُ بَغْيًۢا بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِى بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۱۷)
and We gave them manifest precepts. But they did not differ except after knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves. Indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. (17)

Key Points

With the miracles of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), his truthfulness became clear to the people, and with the signs of the Prophet of Islam, which were clearly mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel, the truthfulness of the Prophet became clear to the people. «آتیناهم بیّنات من الامر»

Messages

1- Allah completes the proof (Hujjah) upon the people. «و آتیناهم بیّنات من الامر»

2- The highest form of ingratitude (Kufrān) is disagreement regarding the truth. Although God gave them superiority and dominance over the enemy, «آتینا - رزقنا - فضلنا», they disagreed instead of showing gratitude. «فما اختلفوا»

3- The Quran's reproach of past generations is a lesson for future generations. «آتیناهم بیّنات... اختلفوا... بَغیاً بینهم»

4- Knowledge alone is not salvific (saving). How many are those who know but disagree due to envy (or jealousy). «من بعد ما جاءهم العلم»

5- Disagreement after knowledge is transgression (Baghy) and oppression (Ẓulm). «بغیاً»

6- The Day of Judgment is the day for resolving disagreements. «یقضى بینهم یوم القیامة فیما كانوا فیه یختلفون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَىٰ شَرِيعَةٍ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ فَٱتَّبِعْهَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۸)
Then We set you upon a clear course of the Law; so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know. (18)

Key Points

«شریعة» (Shari'ah) is a path for accessing water, located beside large rivers where direct access to the water is not possible. Since the rulings of the religion are a path for reaching true life, they are also called Shari'ah.

Messages

1- The determination of the Shari'ah, which is the path of religiosity, is in the hands of Allah. «جعلناك على شریعة»

2- The one responsible for guiding and instructing others must have complete insight and mastery over his path. «على شریعة» (Meaning the Prophet has mastery over the path and the Shari'ah.)

3- The mission (Risalah) of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is a continuation of the mission of Prophet Moses. (In the two previous verses, the discussion was about the prophethood, wisdom, and heavenly Book of the Children of Israel.) «ثمّ جعلناك»

4- The spirit of the religions is one, although there are differences in the devotional programs. (The word «شریعة» (Shari'ah) is indefinite, which indicates the existence of multiple paths, but the word «الامر» (The Command/Affair) is definite, meaning all paths return to a single Command/Affair.) «شریعة من الامر»

5- The emergence of distortion and disagreement is the context for the coming of a new Prophet. (The previous verse spoke of the disagreements of the Children of Israel; this verse speaks of the advent of the Prophet of Islam.) «اختلفوا... ثمّ جعلناك على شریعة»

6- The Prophet, like other people, must also follow the Shari'ah. «على شریعة فاتبعها»

7- If the path is not the path of Allah, it is the path of one's own desires or the desires of others. «شریعة... فاتبعها ولا تتّبع اهواء...»

8- The leader must be firm and should not succumb to the pressure of ignorant elements or foolish suggestions. «و لا تتّبع اهواء الّذین لا یعلمون»

9- The desires of the people are so prominent that they place even the Prophet at risk. «ولا تتّبع اهواء الّذین لا یعلمون»

10- Knowledge and ignorance have an effect on human inclinations. «اهواء الّذین لا یعلمون»

11- Knowledge and learning lead man toward following the Divine Shari'ah. Just as following instincts and desires (hawa) is a sign of ignorance and foolishness. «شریعة... فاتبعها و لا تتّبع... الّذین لا یعلمون»

Ayahs

إِنَّهُمْ لَن يُغْنُوا۟ عَنكَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ بَعْضٍ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ وَلِىُّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۹)
Indeed they will not avail you in any way against Allah. Indeed the wrongdoers are allies of one another, but Allah is the ally of the Godwary. (19)
هَـٰذَا بَصَـٰٓئِرُ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (۲٠)
These are eye-openers for mankind, and guidance and mercy for a people who have certainty. (20)

Key Points

Allah is All-Seeing (All-Aware). «كان اللّه بما تعملون بصیرا»(12) The Messenger of God is also All-Seeing (possesses insight). «ادعوا الى اللّه على بصیرة»(13) The Quran is also a book of insight. «هذا بصائر» Perhaps the meaning of the Ayah, considering the previous Ayah, is this: O Prophet! Follow the Divine Law (Shari'ah) which is from Allah, and do not follow the desires of the ignorant. Because, in comparison to the Divine Law, statute, and Revelation, the ignorant have no plan or program that could make you independent of Allah's Law and path.

12) Al-Ahzab, 9. 13) Yusuf, 108.

Messages

1- Following ignorant people will not avert any danger when Allah's wrath descends. (The motivation for following people's desires is hoping for their support during difficulties, but the Quran eradicates this notion completely.) «انّهم لن یغنوا عنك من اللّه شیئاً»

2- The affinity of individuals toward one another is the means by which they are attracted to each other. «الظالمین بعضهم اولیاء بعض»

3- Following the desires of the ignorant is equivalent to accepting their guardianship (wilayah) and placing oneself within the orbit of the oppressors. «لا تتّبع اهواء... و انّ الظالمین بعضهم اولیاء بعض»

4- Keep away from the desires of the people and do not worry about being alone. «واللّه ولىّ المتّقین»

5- Piety (Taqwa) is the cause for receiving Divine aid and support. «واللّه ولىّ المتّقین»

6- One of the signs of piety is keeping away from the desires of the people. «واللّه ولىّ المتّقین»

7- The people of piety are far removed from oppression and from accepting oppression. «و انّ الظالمین بعضهم اولیاء بعض و اللّه ولىّ المتّقین»

8- Religious adherence must be based on insight. «هذا بصائر»

9- The Quran is a means of insight in all intellectual, moral, political, social, economic, familial, and other fields. («بصائر» is used in the plural form.)

10- Many matters can only be known through Revelation, and intellect and experience have no access to them. «هذا بصائر»

11- The insight provided by the Quran is for all people. «بصائر للناس» Although benefiting from that insight is only for the people of certainty (yaqin). «هدى و رحمة لقوم یوقنون»

12- The Quran is a comprehensive book. (It both shows the path of truth, «بصائر», and guides man toward it, «هُدىً», and grants man fulfillment through it, «رحمة».)

Ayah

أَمْ حَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱجْتَرَحُوا۟ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ أَن نَّجْعَلَهُمْ كَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ سَوَآءً مَّحْيَاهُمْ وَمَمَاتُهُمْ ۚ سَآءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ (۲۱)
Do those who have perpetrated misdeeds suppose that We shall treat them like those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their life and death being equal? Evil is the judgement that they make! (21)

Key Points

Ijtirāḥ (اجتراح) derives from jarḥ (جرح), meaning an injury or wound inflicted upon the body. Since sin wounds the human soul, the term ijtarahū (اجترحوا) is used regarding those who commit transgressions.

The method of comparison is frequently seen in the Qur'an, including: Is the believer equal to the transgressor? «أفمن كان مؤمناً كمن كان فاسقاً» (14) Are the knowledgeable equal to the ignorant? «هل یستوى الّذین یعلمون والّذین لا یعلمون» (15) Are those among you who spent for Islam before the Conquest (of Mecca) and during the time of hardship equal to those who provided financial aid after the Conquest? «لا یستوى منكم مَن أنفق من قبل الفتح و...» (16)

In this Ayah, we also read: Are the transgressors equal to the believing doers of good? «الّذین اجترحوا السیّئات... كالّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

The Believer and the Disbeliever: In comparing the believer and the disbeliever, we see that the believer is tranquil through the remembrance of Allah, content with what Allah has given, hopeful for the future, his path is clear, and he considers death a journey to a greater abode. But the disbeliever revolves daily around a Tāghūt (tyrant/false deity) and is constantly subject to his own desires or the desires of others; he sees the future as dark and death as annihilation.

Therefore, the Qur'an presents the method of comparison for greater clarification, so that all consciences, at every level, may grasp the issue:

Believers increase in their faith, «زادتهم ایمانا» (17), but the disbelievers increase in their disbelief. «و ازدادوا كفراً» (18)

The Guardian of the believer is Allah, «اللّه ولىّ الّذین آمنوا», but the guardians of others are the Tāghūts. «اولیائهم الطاغوت» (19)

Allah sends down tranquility upon the heart of the believer, «انزل السكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین» (20), but He casts fear and terror into the hearts of the disbelievers. «سنلقى فى قلوب الّذین كفروا الرّعب» (21)

People of faith follow the truth, «الّذین آمنوا اتّبعوا الحقّ», but the disbelievers follow falsehood. «الّذین كفروا اتّبعوا الباطل» (22)

The future of the believer is love and acceptance, «سیجعل لهم الرّحمن ودّا» (23), but the future of the disbeliever is humiliation and disgrace. «ترهقهم ذلة» (24)

People of faith receive the salutations of the angels when their souls are taken, «تتوفّاهم الملائكة طیبین یقولون سلام علیكم» (25), but the oppressors face the wrath of the angels. «تتوفّاهم الملائكة ظالمى انفسهم» (26)

The struggle (Jihād) of the people of faith is in the way of Allah, «یقاتلون فى سبیل اللّه», but the fighting and conflict of the disbelievers is in the way of the Tāghūt. «یقاتلون فى سبیل الطاغوت» (27)

The reward of the people of faith is preserved, «لا نضیع اجر من احسن عملا» (28), but the efforts of others are wiped out. «حبطت اعمالهم» (29)

The believer considers his resources and wealth to be from Allah, «هذا من فضل ربّى» (30), but the disbeliever assumes everything he has is due to his own knowledge, skill, and effort. «انما اوتیته على علم عندى» (31)

The deeds of the believer are enduring, «فلن یضلّ اعمالهم» (32), but the deeds of the disbelievers are lost. «اضلّ اعمالهم» (33)

The believer fears no one, «لا یخشون احداً» (34), but others are fearful. «یخشون الناس كخشیة اللّه» (35)

Angels descend upon the people of faith, «تتنزّل علیهم الملائكة» (36), but devils descend upon others. «تنزّل الشیاطین» (37)

People of faith are hopeful, «یبشّر المؤمنین» (38), but the disbelievers are despairing. «اذا هم یقنطون» (39)

And in a single word, the path of truth is the highest path, «كلمة اللّه هى العلیا», and the path of disbelief is the lowest. «كلمة الّذین كفروا السفلى» (40)

With all these clear Qur'anic signposts, is there any room left for doubt and uncertainty? Indeed, there is no doubt in the religion, «لاریب فیه» (41), but they create hesitation within themselves. «فى ریبهم یترددون» (42)

14) Surah As-Sajdah, 18. 15) Surah Az-Zumar, 9. 16) Surah Al-Hadīd, 10. 17) Surah Al-Anfāl, 2. 18) Surah Āl ‘Imrān, 90. 19) Surah Al-Baqarah, 257. 20) Surah Al-Fatḥ, 4. 21) Surah Al-Anfāl, 12. 22) Surah Muḥammad, 3. 23) Surah Maryam, 96. 24) Surah Yūnus, 27. 25) Surah An-Naḥl, 32. 26) Surah An-Naḥl, 28. 27) Surah An-Nisā’, 76. 28) Surah Al-Kahf, 30. 29) Surah Al-Baqarah, 217. 30) Surah An-Naml, 40. 31) Surah Al-Qaṣaṣ, 78. 32) Surah Muḥammad, 4. 33) Surah Muḥammad, 1. 34) Surah Al-Aḥzāb, 39. 35) Surah An-Nisā’, 77. 36) Surah Fuṣṣilat, 30. 37) Surah Ash-Shu‘arā’, 221. 38) Surah Al-Isrā’, 9. 39) Surah Ar-Rūm, 36. 40) Surah At-Tawbah, 40. 41) Surah Al-Baqarah, 2. 42) Surah At-Tawbah, 45.

Messages

1- Allah is Just, and in His sight, the evildoer and the doer of good are not equal. «ام حسب...»

2- The root of many deviations lies in baseless assumptions. «ام حسب الّذین...»

3- Sin is a danger, but the greatest danger is for those who actively seek out sin. «اجترحوا السیّئات» (The word ijtiraḥ implies seeking and desiring [the action]).

4- Transgressors, by their very nature (fiṭrah), desire to be ranked alongside the doers of good. «ام حسب الّذین اجترحوا السیئات...»

5- Both faith and righteous deeds, as well as sin, play a role in the lives and deaths of human beings. «سواء محیاهم و مماتهم»

6- Judgment based on imagination and assumption is condemned. «ام حسب... ساء ما یحكمون»

Ayah

وَخَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۲۲)
Allah created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom, so that every soul may be requited for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged. (22)

Key Points

This Ayah serves as the proof for the previous Ayah. In the previous Ayah, we read that the transgressors imagine that We will make them like the believers. This Ayah considers that a vain imagination and states: Do they not know that the heavens and the earth were created based on truth (al-Haqq), and in a system of truth, the good and the bad are not equal? Yes, the necessity of creation being based on truth is justice in retribution. Truth (Haqq) is applied to something that is based on wisdom and logic. Creation based on truth means creation based on a plan and a goal. The reason for the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is Divine wisdom and justice, both of which are alluded to in this Ayah. As for wisdom (Hikmah): If man goes to annihilation upon death, creation would be futile, whereas creation is based on truth, wisdom, and purpose. Which potter would be willing to senselessly break his pots after making them? And how can creation be erased by death? As for justice ('Adl): The retribution for every person is given without any injustice being done to him. Yes, if retribution is not given, or if it is given excessively, it is injustice.

Messages

1- The cosmic system is founded upon truth (al-Haqq); therefore, man will also be treated according to truth. «خلق... بالحقّ... لتجزى»

2- The establishment of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) and just reward and retribution are necessities of creation. (The letter waw in «و لتجزى» indicates that creation pursues goals, one of which is reward and retribution.)

3- The system of this world and the Hereafter is based on truth and justice. «خلق... بالحق... و لتجزى... و هم لا یظلمون»

4- The basis of recompense is action, not hope or desire. «لتجزى... بما كسبت»

Ayah

أَفَرَءَيْتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَـٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ سَمْعِهِۦ وَقَلْبِهِۦ وَجَعَلَ عَلَىٰ بَصَرِهِۦ غِشَـٰوَةً فَمَن يَهْدِيهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۲۳)
Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god and whom Allah has led astray knowingly, set a seal upon his hearing and heart, and put a blindfold on his sight? So who will guide him after Allah [has consigned him to error]? Will you not then take admonition? (23)

Key Points

The Quran uses the phrase «ختم اللّه» (Allah has sealed) regarding two groups: one is the disbelievers (kuffar), about whom He says: «ختم اللّه على قلوبهم و على سمعهم» (43); and the other is the followers of desires (hawa-parastan), about whom He says: «ختم اللّه على سمعه و قلبه». The meaning of following desires (hawa-parasti) is neglecting duty and following instinct. Following desires disables the tools of recognition; the eye neither sees the truth, nor does the ear hear the truth, nor does the heart have a correct understanding. One of the factors leading to the following of desires is the inclination towards materialism. As we read elsewhere: «اخلد الى الارض و اتبع هواه» (44). Following desires is the basis of afflictions: «الهوى اسّ المحن» (45). Desires are the vehicle of tribulations. «انما بدء وقوع الفتن اهواء تتبع» (46). The source of tribulations is the following of desires. Following desires prevents justice. «فلا تتّبع الهوى ان تعدلوا» (47). It causes deviation from the path of Allah. «ولا تتّبع الهوى فیضلّك عن سبیل اللّه» (48). It causes the downfall of man. «واتّبع هواه فتردى» (49). And in this Ayah, we read that following desires causes the sealing of the ear and the heart. «اتّخذ اللّه هواه... و ختم على سمعه و قلبه».

43) Baqarah, 7. 44) A'raf, 176. 45) Ghurar al-Hikam. 46) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 54. 47) Nisa, 135. 48) Sad, 26. 49) Taha, 16.

Messages

1- Following desires (hawa-parasti) is a type of polytheism (shirk), and the follower of desires deserves reproach. «أفرایت من اتّخذ الهه هواه»

2- Although desires are internal, the reflection of following desires is evident in human actions. «افرایت»

3- Man is inherently a worshipping being, but he deviates in the choice of the object of worship, turning to desires instead of Allah. «اتّخذ الهه هواه»

4- The punishment for one who abandons Allah and follows his carnal desires is that Allah misguides him. «اضلّه اللّه»

5- If the following of desires dominates, knowledge loses its effectiveness. Following desires misguides even the learned. «و اضلّه اللّه على علم»

6- Misguidance and spiritual blindness are the Divine punishment for the followers of desires. «اتّخذ الهه هواه... اضلّه اللّه... ختم على سمعه و قلبه»

7- Following desires causes the doors of guidance to be closed upon man. «مَن اتّخذ الهه هواه... فمن یهدیه»

8- Except for Divine guidance, every path leads to deviation (dalalah). «فمن یهدیه من بعد اللّه»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ مَا هِىَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنْيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيَا وَمَا يُهْلِكُنَآ إِلَّا ٱلدَّهْرُ ۚ وَمَا لَهُم بِذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ۖ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ (۲٤)
They say, ‘There is nothing but the life of this world: we live and we die, and nothing but time destroys us.’ But they do not have any knowledge of that, and they only make conjectures. (24)

Key Points

Dahr means the passage of worldly time. It seems the materialists believe that the passage of life is the cause of man's death and destruction. (50)

50) Mufradāt Rāghib.

Messages

1- Following desires blinds man's eyes and heart so much that he denies the Resurrection and confines life to the short worldly existence. «اتّخذ الهه هواه... حیاتنا الدنیا»

2- The deniers of the Hereafter have no logic or knowledge for their denial. «مالهم بذلك من علم»

3- In matters of belief, conjecture and assumption are worthless; beliefs must be based on knowledge. «ان هم الا یظنون»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ مَّا كَانَ حُجَّتَهُمْ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوا۟ ٱئْتُوا۟ بِـَٔابَآئِنَآ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۲٥)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, their only argument is to say, ‘Bring our fathers back [to life], if you are truthful.’ (25)
قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يَجْمَعُكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۲٦)
Say, ‘It is Allah who gives you life, then He makes you die. Then He will gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.’ (26)

Key Points

The system of creation has a plan and a law that the Wise Allah has made sovereign over existence, and denying it is a sign of ignorance and foolishness.

Messages

1- The verses of the Quran are clear and illuminating. «آیاتنا بیّنات»

2- Instead of reflecting upon the verses of Allah, the disbelievers demand their fathers and forefathers. «تتلى علیهم آیاتنا... ائتونا بآبائنا»

3- Whenever you quote the words of the deniers, state the response to it as well. «و ما یهلكنا الا الدهر... قل اللّه یحییكم»

4- The signs of Allah's power, wisdom, and justice are so clear and manifest that they leave no room for doubt concerning the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). «لا ریب فیه»

5- The initial life of mankind is proof of the possibility of the Day of Resurrection. «یحییكم... ثمّ یحییكم»

6- The request to resurrect ancestors in this world is based on ignorance and unawareness. «اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»

7- Doubt concerning the Resurrection (Ma'ad) stems from human ignorance. «اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَخْسَرُ ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ (۲۷)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and when the Hour sets in, the falsifiers will be losers on that day. (27)

Messages

1- Giving life and causing death is easy for Allah, because existence is in His hand. «یحییكم... یمیتكم... للّه ملك السموات...»

2- Worldly losses are insignificant; the loss on the Day of Resurrection is important. «یوم تقوم الساعة یومئذ یخسر المبطلون»

3- Those who consider the Resurrection void will be the losers on the Day of Resurrection. «یومئذ یخسر المبطلون»

Ayah

وَتَرَىٰ كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جَاثِيَةً ۚ كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ تُدْعَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ كِتَـٰبِهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۸)
And you will see every nation fallen on its knees. Every nation will be summoned to its book: ‘Today you will be requited for what you used to do. (28)

Key Points

"Jāthiyah" (جاثیه) means sitting on the knees, at the time of fear and terror, or humility and submission. The Quran has introduced three types of books (records) for humanity on the Day of Resurrection: (51) A) For every individual, there is a book in which his personal deeds are recorded. «و كلّ انسان الزمناه طائره فى عنقه و نخرج له یوم القیامة كتاباً یلقاه منشورا» (52) We attach the deed of every human being to his neck, and on the Day of Resurrection, We will unfold it before him as a book, and he will be addressed: Read your book. «اقرء كتابك» (53) B) For every nation (Ummah), there is also a book in which social matters are recorded, as He says in this Ayah: «كلّ امّة تدعى الى كتابها» C) All nations also have one book in which everything and everyone is recorded. «و وضع الكتاب فترى المجرمین مشفقین ممّا فیه» (54)

51) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 13, p. 348. 52) Al-Isra, 13. 53) Al-Isra, 14. 54) Al-Kahf, 49.

Messages

1- The terror and dread of the Day of Resurrection will bring everyone to their knees (paralyze them). «كلّ امّة جاثیة»

2- The Resurrection is physical (bodily). «جاثیة»

3- The system of existence has accountability, reckoning, and recording, and the affairs of the Resurrection are based on records and witnesses. «تدعى الى كتابها»

4- The recompense of every person on the Day of Resurrection is based on his performance in the world. «الیوم تجزون ما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

هَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبُنَا يَنطِقُ عَلَيْكُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا نَسْتَنسِخُ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۹)
This is Our book, which speaks truly against you. Indeed We used to record what you used to do.’ (29)

Key Points

The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said: When a person remembers Allah in his heart, Allah records it in a book and reminds the angels. They say: We have no knowledge (of this). Allah says: I record his remembrance of the heart as well. (55) We also read in Du'a Kumayl: «و الشاهد لما خفى عنهم» (And the Witness to what is hidden from them). O Lord! You are aware of what is concealed from the angels.

55) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, nothing is hidden; everything is present. «هذا»

2- On the Day of Judgment, everything is recorded. «كتاب»

3- The record of human deeds is precise, corresponds to reality, and is in no need of explanation or interpretation. «ینطق علیكم بالحقّ»

4- The reason for the precision and truthfulness of the book of human deeds is that Allah Himself recorded it. «انّا كنّا نستنسخ»

5- Belief in the precise recording of deeds can be the best deterrent against wrongdoing for humans. «كنا نستنسخ»

6- A person's deed is recorded exactly as it occurred, not based on circumstantial evidence, witnesses, or effects. «عما كنتم تعملون»

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَيُدْخِلُهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۳٠)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the manifest success! (30)
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَفَلَمْ تَكُنْ ءَايَـٰتِى تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَٱسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ وَكُنتُمْ قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ (۳۱)
But as for the faithless, [they will be asked,] ‘Were not My signs recited to you? But you were disdainful, and you were a guilty lot. (31)

Key Points

Fawz (فوز) means victory, achieving goodness accompanied by safety, and reaching one's aspirations. The word fawz (فوز) appears in the Quran accompanied by the words mubīn (manifest), azīm (great), and kabīr (immense). In this Ayah, instead of saying yadkhulūna fī al-jannah (they enter Paradise), He said: «یدخلهم ربّهم فى رحمته» (their Lord will admit them into His Mercy). This means that the people of faith and action are within the sphere of Allah's special grace and Mercy. Alongside faith (īmān), righteous action (amal sālih) is mentioned, but alongside disbelief (kufr), no good deed is mentioned. In the Arabic language, if plural words come with alif and lām (the definite article 'al-'), it signifies all individuals (or instances). For example, the word masājid (مساجد) means mosques, but the word al-masājid (المساجد) means all the mosques. Usually in the Quran, alongside faith, the discussion is about righteous action, specifically in the form of «الصالحات» (al-sālihāt), meaning all the deeds of the believer must be good.

Messages

1- The successful person is one who possesses righteous deeds alongside faith, and whose righteous deeds are extensive and comprehensive. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

2- Receiving Divine Mercy is not possible without faith and righteous action. «فامّا الّذین آمنوا... فى رحمته»

3- Reward and punishment are among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «یدخلهم ربّهم فى رحمته»

4- Paradise and its blessings are a manifestation of Divine Mercy. «یدخلهم ربّهم فى رحمته»

5- The successful person is one who has a good ultimate outcome. «ذلك هوالفوز»

6- Man naturally desires success; it is the Quran that shows man true success. «ذلك هو الفوز المبین»

7- The root of disbelief (kufr) is arrogance (takabbur). «كفروا... استكبرتم»

8- Immediate arrogance (upon hearing the truth) is a sign of obstinacy. «تتلى علیكم فاستكبرتم»

9- The root of crime and sin is disbelief and arrogance. «كفروا... استكبرتم... مجرمین»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا قِيلَ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَٱلسَّاعَةُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَا قُلْتُم مَّا نَدْرِى مَا ٱلسَّاعَةُ إِن نَّظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنًّا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُسْتَيْقِنِينَ (۳۲)
When it was said, ‘‘Allah’s promise is indeed true, and there is no doubt about the Hour,’’ you said, ‘‘We do not know what the Hour is. We know nothing beyond conjectures, and we do not possess any certainty.’’ ’ (32)
وَبَدَا لَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۳۳)
The evils of what they had done will appear to them, and they will be besieged by what they used to deride. (33)
وَقِيلَ ٱلْيَوْمَ نَنسَىٰكُمْ كَمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَا وَمَأْوَىٰكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۳٤)
And it will be said, ‘Today We will forget you, just as you forgot the encounter of this day of yours. The Fire will be your abode, and you will not have any helpers. (34)

Key Points

The sign of the disbeliever is doubt, and the sign of the believer is questioning, follow-up, and reaching certainty. Sometimes a small transgression leads to many harms. For example, a person who turns off the light in a hall apparently commits one wrong act, but this act entails many evils (سيّئات). People become afraid and terrified due to the darkness, some lose their shoes and clothes, a group tramples others, and some run into columns. It is possible that thefts and other forbidden acts occur in the darkness. All these matters are the negative effects and consequences of a single action. Therefore, on the Day of Judgment, accountability will be made for all the consequences of the sin. The meaning of Allah forgetting them is that on the Day of Judgment, those who were heedless of the Resurrection will be treated like forgotten people, and Allah will abandon them to their own state. Otherwise, Allah never forgets anyone or anything. «و ما كان ربّك نسيا» (56)

56) Maryam, 64.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the day when hidden matters are revealed and manifested. «و بدا لهم»

2- On the Day of Judgment, in addition to their deeds, the consequences, effects, and corruptions resulting from those deeds will also afflict man. «سيّئات ما عملوا»

3- Divine punishment is commensurate with human behavior. The very thing they used to mock will afflict them. «حاق بهم ما كانوا به يستهزؤن»

4- Mockery is the method of the arrogant. «استكبرتم... كانوا يستهزؤن»

5- Whoever forgets the Resurrection will be forgotten on the Day of Judgment. «ننساكم كما نسيتم»

6- Belief in the Resurrection is not enough; remembrance of the Resurrection is necessary. «نسيتم لقاء يومكم»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكُم بِأَنَّكُمُ ٱتَّخَذْتُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ هُزُوًا وَغَرَّتْكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ فَٱلْيَوْمَ لَا يُخْرَجُونَ مِنْهَا وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ (۳٥)
That is because you took the signs of Allah in derision, and the life of the world had deceived you.’ So today, they will not be brought out of it, nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah]. (35)

Key Points

Istī‘tāb (استعتاب) means requesting something that removes distress and sorrow, similar to seeking forgiveness (or making an excuse). In these few verses that have passed, an aspect of the Court of Resurrection has been depicted: 1. Everyone will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection. «یجمعكم» 2. The followers of falsehood will see their loss on that Day. «یخسر المبطلون» 3. Every nation will be called towards its book. «كلّ امّة تدعى الى كتابها» 4. The book is a witness to the deeds. «هذا كتابنا ینطق علیكم بالحقّ» 5. People will be divided into two groups: one group in mercy and another group destined for Hellfire. 6. The reasons for the punishment will be explained to the inhabitants of Hell. «استكبرتم... اجترحوا السیئات» 7. The punishment is commensurate with the crime. «ننساكم كما نسیتم لقاء یومكم هذا» 8. The consequences and evil effects of sins will take hold, and the path of exit and excuse-making is closed. The most important factor leading to wretchedness is being deceived by the world, mockery, and doubt.

Messages

1- State the reason for the punishment to the criminal. «ذلكم بانكم...»

2- Becoming joyful, proud, and intoxicated by the life of this world leads a person to mock the truths. «اتّخذتم آیات اللّه هزوا»

3- The world itself is not criticized, but rather being deceived by it is criticized. «غرّتكم الحیاة الدنیا»

4- For those who belittle and mock the heavenly doctrine, there is neither a path to salvation nor a way to make or have excuses accepted. «لا یخرجون... لا یستعتبون»

Ayahs

فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْحَمْدُ رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَرَبِّ ٱلْأَرْضِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۳٦)
So all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, the Lord of all the worlds. (36)
وَلَهُ ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۳۷)
To Him belongs all supremacy in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (37)

Key Points

In the beginning of the Surah, where the discussion was about the revelation of the Quran and blessings, Allah was introduced as the Mighty and the Wise: «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز الحكیم». And at the end, where the discussion is about the punishment of the mockers, the expression Aziz (Mighty) and Hakim (Wise) is also used. Perhaps this indicates that blessings, punishments, mercies, and wrath all arise from Divine power and wisdom.

Messages

1- Only Allah is worthy of greatness, praise, and glorification. «فللّه الحمد»

2- The Lord of the heavens, the earth, and all existence is the One God. «فللّه الحمد ربّ... ربّ... ربّ»

3- The heavens, the earth, and everything are under Divine nurturing and moving towards growth. «ربّ السموات...»

4- The might and power of Allah are accompanied by His wisdom and competence. «العزیز الحكیم»
